mechanism_narrative: Rising real interest rates compress equity valuation multiples by increasing the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during periods of rising FEDFUNDS, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals immediate labor market deterioration and recession risk, triggering rapid equity sell-offs within the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or signal loses predictive power after 2020.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate revenue growth, driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity and weakening economic momentum, resulting in negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 20-day returns in > 50% of observations, or regime-specific alpha vanishes in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 5.0% imposes severe financing constraints on highly leveraged firms, triggering immediate margin calls and short-term equity depreciation.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of periods when FEDFUNDS stays above 5.0%, or signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating positive quarterly returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction effect Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly translates to increased corporate profit expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no significant correlation with 20-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production dropping below its 12-month moving average signals acute supply-side contraction and inventory depletion, causing rapid short-term equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances when INDPRO falls below its 12-month average, or signal loses edge after 2015.
